follow_up_question: Can you name another famous character Kenny Ascher worked with besides Kermit the Frog?

title: B-52's Drummer Rocks into the World
story: Boom! The beat of rock music got a little louder on October 26, 1953. That's when Julian Keith Strickland was born in Athens, Georgia. He grew up to become the talented drummer for the B-52's, a fun and quirky band. Strickland helped create their catchy tunes, including the famous song 'Rock Lobster'.
activity_question: What famous song did Keith Strickland help create with the B-52's?
follow_up_question: Can you think of any other bands named after objects like the B-52's?

title: A Kingdom Splits in Two
story: Imagine two countries joined together, then deciding to go their separate ways. That's what happened when Sweden recognized Norway as its own country on October 26, 1905. Before this, they were united as one kingdom. Now, Norway could make its own choices with its own king. It was a peaceful change, showing how nations can grow apart but still be friendly neighbors.
activity_question: What did Sweden recognize Norway as in 1905?
follow_up_question: Can you think of other countries that used to be united but are now separate?

title: Shaken City: Constantinople's Earthquake
story: The ground rumbled and buildings swayed as a powerful earthquake struck Constantinople in 740. On October 26, the bustling city felt nature's might. The quake damaged the famous city walls that had protected Constantinople for centuries. Many buildings cracked and crumbled. Despite the destruction, the resilient people began rebuilding, determined to restore their great city.
activity_question: What part of Constantinople was damaged in the earthquake?
follow_up_question: Why were city walls important to ancient cities like Constantinople?

title: Minutemen: America's Quick-Response Heroes
story: Danger was brewing in the American colonies. On October 26, 1774, brave colonists formed special teams called Minutemen. These swift soldiers could grab their weapons and be ready to fight in just 60 seconds! They played a crucial role in the upcoming American Revolution, defending their homes and freedom against British rule.
activity_question: How quickly could Minutemen be ready to fight?
bonus_fact: The name 'Minutemen' comes from their ability to be ready 'at a minute's notice.'

title: Kicking Off a New Era in Soccer
story: Imagine a world where soccer had no standard rules! That changed in 1863 when a group of English sports enthusiasts gathered on October 26. They formed the Football Association, creating the first official rulebook for soccer. This meeting separated soccer from rugby, giving birth to the beautiful game we know today.
activity_question: What organization was formed to standardize soccer rules?
bonus_fact: Before 1863, different schools and clubs played by their own unique soccer rules!

title: A Secret Path Under the Thames
story: Imagine walking beneath a mighty river! In 1912, the Woolwich Foot Tunnel opened on October 26, creating a hidden pathway under the Thames in England. This underwater walkway allowed people to cross the river without using a boat. It was like a secret passage, stretching deep below the water's surface, connecting two sides of London in a whole new way.
activity_question: What opened on October 26, 1912?
follow_up_question: Why do you think people might prefer using a tunnel instead of a boat to cross a river?

title: Mustang Takes to the Skies
story: Zoom! A sleek new fighter plane roared into the air for the first time. It was 1940, and the P-51 Mustang had just made its maiden flight on October 26. This powerful aircraft would soon become famous for its speed and long-range abilities. Pilots loved flying the Mustang, which played a crucial role in World War II.
activity_question: What type of aircraft made its maiden flight in this story?
bonus_fact: The P-51 Mustang could fly for hours without refueling, making it perfect for long missions.
